reviews.review_msg: His lectures, LO's, Quizzes, and practice questions often conflict, as material asked in the questions aren't covered in lectures, which stressed me on how to study. Exams are based entirely on module review questions, and lowkey were one of the easiest exams i ever did in my life. In person, he is a super chill guy. Just lectures are weird

I studied quite a bit for his class but the amount of content in this course makes me question the 100 level difficulty. There's just so much material to know and he'll exam you on the smallest details. It's an online class but he acts like it doesn't even exist. He barely communicated throughout the semester. Be ready to put in 10 hours a week.

Pro
+ Everything was VERY organized & accessible from lectures, practice methods, criteria, etc.

Con
- SO much reading. Textbook is mandatory for even a glimpse of success. 

Success is dependant on time you're willing to provide. Only 10% get A- or above. I only did well because I studied the content 3-8 hours a week. He himself was great though!

I took this course as a prerequisite. The professor provides you with everything you need to pass the course. Use his audio lectures as they are extremely helpful and will take time off the textbook. Though, the amount of material is way too much for a level 100 course. TA will also take off marks for something unrelated to the question.

I think he has changed the way he teaches by A LOT! I started doing better in the class when I focused on his lecture slides which saved so much time from the textbook. He practically gives you everything you should know/study for the exams on the modules provided.

Class itself honestly isn't that bad. I found that making notes for the textbook and watching the lecture slides after really helped to cut down what you needed to know. However, I found that assignments were graded very harshly and marks were docked off if you missed even the slightest detail.

Get ready to read 90% of the textbook to do well on assignments and exams. Lecture slides are long and boring. BPK 105 goes into extreme detail about different systems within the human body. TA is a tough grader as well, you'd meet all the criteria on assignment rubrics and still get marks docked for missing something unrelated to the question.

Professor Dill is not a bad prof himself, however, bpk105 coursework is challenging. It is textbook-heavy, to Ace exams you practically have to memorize everything on the pages of the textbook assigned. If you aren't good with memorization, I wouldn't recommend it. The topics go into EXTREME DETAIL about each of the body systems! not an elective!!!
